danger_signals.visual_form: Rival company track placement on map; competing trains on routes
danger_signals.meaning: Economic threat; competitors attempting to undercut player's profitability and market share

danger_signals.visual_form: Bandits appearing on map; visual indicators on affected train routes
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to cargo delivery; trains can be hijacked and cargo lost

danger_signals.visual_form: Pricing indicators; rival company price undercutting displayed in route information
danger_signals.meaning: Economic competition threat; rivals reducing rates to capture cargo contracts

danger_signals.visual_form: Financial display showing rising debt and interest rates
danger_signals.meaning: Economic danger; growing financial burden from initial loan startup capital

safety_signals.visual_form: Connected railroad lines on map; owned stations linked by player-laid track
safety_signals.meaning: Operational security; reliable cargo transportation infrastructure in place

safety_signals.visual_form: Sheriff units visible on map; stationed at stations or along routes
safety_signals.meaning: Protection against bandit hijacking; active defense of cargo

safety_signals.visual_form: Financial display showing increasing equity vs. debt ratio
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; company becoming self-sufficient and profitable

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple active train routes connecting different industries and destinations
safety_signals.meaning: Market resilience; revenue diversification reduces dependency on single cargo type

faction_indicators.color_identity: Variable (player-selectable company color in various versions)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Company name/logo displayed on owned track sections and stations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: All track, stations, and trains bearing player's company color/name throughout map

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct colors for each AI competitor (red, blue, green, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Competitor company names displayed on their track and trains
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Competing track networks; different colored trains; rival stations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Separate track sections and station networks; presence on same routes indicates rate war competition

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mobile threat units that appear on routes and stations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in specific regional content (Africa scenarios in Deluxe)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sheriff units visually distinguished from bandits
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Positioned to intercept and arrest bandits
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stationed at player-controlled stations or patrolling along routes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse track layout; few stations; small train roster; limited map coverage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High vulnerability to competitor tactics; limited route options; slow cargo movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanding connected track network; multiple stations across regions; increasing number of active trains
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability to engage in rate wars; competitive pricing responses; route diversification

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dominant track network covering major regions; numerous stations; large fleet of trains; visual dominance on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Market control; ability to dictate cargo rates; competitors marginalized or eliminated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Financial display showing red/negative numbers; potential reduction in active trains and routes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited ability to expand; vulnerability to competitor pressure; potential bankruptcy threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bandit markers appearing on map; affected routes show cargo loss indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cargo hijacking; need to deploy sheriffs; alternative routing becomes necessary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Industries visible on map (mines, farms, factories); cargo type availability indicated at stations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cargo demand and pricing varies by region; route profitability depends on connecting supply to demand

environmental_storytelling.example: Different maps represent historical settings (U.S. West, Northeast, England, Europe, Africa, South America); each region has region-specific cargo types (troops for Africa) and industrial configurations that tell story of economic activity

First century vs. second century campaigns; expanded time ranges and locomotive types in later versions show technological progression of railroad industry; futuristic scenarios in Railroad Tycoon II show speculative world state (raised ocean levels)
Placement of mines, farms, factories, and population centers on map implicitly communicates regional economy; player must identify profitable cargo connections based on this industrial geography
Rival railroad networks visible on map communicate competitive landscape; their expansion patterns and route choices show strategic competition for market dominance
Bandit and sheriff units in Deluxe version establish danger narrative in certain regions; their presence communicates lawlessness and need for active defense in frontier territories

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reduces cargo rates on overlapping routes; places trains on same lines; attempts stock manipulation
Reduced train activity; limited track expansion; potential withdrawal from routes
Appears on map; targets trains carrying cargo; hijacks shipments causing cargo loss
Patrols routes or stations; intercepts and arrests bandits; provides protection to assigned train routes
Regular scheduled movement; consistent cargo delivery; revenue generation
May be discontinued; resources redirected to better-performing routes; removal from schedule
Aggressive track expansion; competitive rate responses; stock price appreciation
Reduced expansion; defensive pricing; potential forced asset sales

notes: Railroad Tycoon is a business simulation with minimal real-time danger but significant economic threat systems. Manifestation of world-state occurs primarily through: (1) map visualization showing network expansion and competitor presence, (2) financial displays communicating economic health, (3) cargo and pricing systems indicating market conditions, and (4) in later versions (Deluxe onward), addition of tactical threats (bandits/sheriffs) adding direct danger signaling. The game communicates faction identity through track color coding and company name labeling rather than through visual stylization. Political/competitive situations manifest as visible rate wars, stock dealings, and spatial competition for market control via track placement. Wikipedia source provides overview of game features but lacks detailed visual communication specifics; extracted data based on documented game mechanics and their logical visual manifestations.
